parentQuestion: A patient with severe diarrhea loses massive amounts of water and electrolytes. ...

scaffolds.q: Osmosis causes water to move toward areas of ___ solute concentration.
scaffolds.explain: Water moves toward HIGHER solute concentration (which is equivalently LOWER water concentration). Think: water dilutes concentrated solutions by flowing toward them. Osmosis is water's attempt to equalize concentrations. Elegant physics.

scaffolds.q: A co-transporter moves ___ molecules across the membrane simultaneously.
scaffolds.explain: A co-transporter (also called a symporter) moves TWO OR MORE different molecules in the SAME direction across the membrane. Example: SGLT1 moves sodium and glucose together. This allows cells to use one molecule's gradient to power another's transport. Exquisite coupling, isn't it?
